long_description:
IPMI cable was plugged back in
Incident source: https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/METAL-7765 - Mark Rogalski
short_description: Need to re-run reclaim after hardware fix
close_notes:
Issue Summary: The incident is related to a bare-metal server issue, where the IPMI cable was plugged back in, and a re-run of the reclaim process is needed after a hardware fix.
SME Findings: The incident was caused by a hardware issue, and the SME (Subject Matter Expert) has determined that a re-run of the reclaim process is necessary to resolve the issue.
Reason of Escalation: The reason for escalation is not explicitly stated, but it appears that the incident was escalated due to the need for a re-run of the reclaim process, which requires additional attention and resources.
Resolution Steps: The resolution steps include re-running the reclaim process using CFN automation.
Signature of Issue: The signature of the issue is a bare-metal server issue with a hardware fix, requiring a re-run of the reclaim process.
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: The incident is related to a Jira ticket (METAL-7765) and a GitHub event (no specific event ID mentioned).
Category/Subcategory: The category is Compute - Development, and the subcategory is not explicitly stated.
Timeline Summary: The incident started on 2025-12-08 at 00:00:00 UTC, and the current status is in progress.
Resolution Runbook: The resolution runbook is not explicitly stated, but it appears that the CFN automation will be used to re-run the reclaim process.
Caused by Change/Incident: The incident was not caused by a change, but rather by a hardware issue.
Warroom: There is no war room mentioned in the context.
Meeting URL: There is no meeting URL mentioned in the context.
Outage Start: The outage started on 2025-12-08 at 00:00:00 UTC.
Outage End
current_status_next_steps:
Use cFN automation to re-run reclaim on tor3-qz1-sr1-rk162-s35
